title: Harden platform merge support design after critical review

sources.reason: Contains the candidate taxonomy, adapter mappings, evidence conclusions, failure handling, tests, and rollout model under critical review.
Records the immediately preceding support-boundary revision and its verified scope.
Defines the canonical Cockpit Recommendation and ready_for_review terminology that the design must not overload.
Official GitHub source for merge_group event activity and candidate SHA semantics.
Official GitHub source for Merge Queue candidate construction, required checks, and queue behavior.
Official GitLab source for Merge Train behavior, enforcement, and the skip-without-restart exception.
Official GitLab source for merge request event type and candidate-bound SHA variables.
Independent accuracy, completeness, consistency, clarity, and audience review identified the evidence-model defects addressed here.

riskAssessment.reason: This is documentation-only, but an incorrect evidence model could cause future adapters to claim parallel-merge safety from stale or policy-bypassed candidates.

executionDecision.reason: The user requested a defect-free critical review; official platform documentation and independent review findings provide sufficient evidence for a documentation-only hardening pass.

preReviewWarnings: Do not let candidate construction mode imply freshness; derive the permitted conclusion from separate evidence dimensions.
Do not overload Cockpit ready_for_review or other governance recommendations with merge-candidate safety.
Treat GitLab skip_merge_train and unenforced train bypass as invalidating policy evidence.
Bind every native adapter base SHA to the platform-generated candidate rather than fetching a newer target ref.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The design models candidate construction and freshness as separate platform-neutral dimensions and maps every combination to an unambiguous, fail-closed conclusion.

The design defines a future candidate-evidence contract with authoritative identity fields, validation and invalidation rules, recording boundary, and command result semantics without claiming that runtime support already exists.
GitHub Merge Queue and GitLab Merge Train adapter mappings identify authoritative candidate-bound inputs; a freshly fetched target ref is never substituted for the base used to build a candidate.
GitLab Merge Train produces a merge-safe conclusion only when enforcement and no-bypass policy evidence is present, including protection against skip_merge_train without restart.
Cockpit ready_for_review remains a Work Item governance recommendation and is not used as a merge-safety conclusion.
The failure lifecycle, negative and race test strategy, and rollout gates cover stale, missing, mismatched, bypassed, interrupted, or unclassifiable evidence with fail-closed outcomes.
GitLab Free remains a concise, explicit non-correspondence statement with head-only diagnostics and no synthetic compatibility protocol.
No runtime scripts, tests, templates, CI files, Makefile targets, or unrelated documentation are changed.
guidelines: Use official platform documentation for behavioral and tier claims.
Keep current implementation facts distinct from future design requirements.
Keep AI Cockpit Core platform-neutral and derive conclusions from explicit evidence rather than platform labels.
Use canonical glossary terms and preserve the existing Work Item Recommendation semantics.
Keep unsupported editions outside the detailed compatibility protocol.

intent.problem: Critical review found that the design conflates candidate construction with freshness, omits an executable evidence contract, overloads ready_for_review, and overstates GitLab Merge Train safety despite documented bypass behavior.
constraints: Correct the design specification only; do not implement adapters, CI, scripts, schemas, or installer behavior.
Preserve the user-approved GitLab Free non-correspondence boundary and avoid reintroducing a synthetic compatibility design.
Base platform claims on official GitHub and GitLab documentation.
Keep prior archived Work Item evidence immutable.
Fail closed whenever candidate identity, freshness, policy enforcement, or event correlation is missing or contradictory.
intent.rationale: Separating evidence dimensions and specifying authoritative adapter inputs prevents future implementations from converting a platform label or target-integrated commit into an unsupported merge-safe claim.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A target-integrated candidate lacks terminal freshness proof.
evidence: Critical completeness and consistency review findings dated 2026-07-15

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A GitLab Merge Train is bypassed or skip_merge_train avoids pipeline restart.
Candidate, base, source, event, or policy evidence is missing, stale, mismatched, delayed, or unclassifiable.
evidence: Official GitHub and GitLab event documentation listed in sources

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Runtime and CI implementation remain outside this documentation Work Item.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item's design-document changes while preserving the prior archived support-boundary Work Item.
